mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer spending power that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive mean return over 60 days when both series expand, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect supply-side bottlenecks that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where industrial production contracts by more than 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate creates real yield compression that forces equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains flat
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a positive mean 20-day return, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC drops below 0.01, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with payroll growth direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in housing starts below 1.4 million units signals collapsing construction demand that triggers immediate sector rotation and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of months where housing starts fall below the 1.4 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase confirms capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations during confirmed recovery phases despite rising industrial production.
